---
title: "Tests for data-generating mechanisms"
author: "Stacy Cyrille"
date: last-modified
format: 
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  # html:
  #   keep-md: false
  #   toc: true
  #   toc-location: left
  #   number-sections: true
  #   embed-resources: true
execute: 
  # echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
fig-width: 8
fig-align: "center"
fig-cap-location: top
html-math-method: mathml
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(MASS)
library(knitr)
library(survival)
library(timereg)

source(here::here("R", "utils.R"))
source(here::here("R", "generate_data.R"))
```

# DNA pathways

**Example 1**

We generate a simple example.

-   Sample size: $n = 1000$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $p = 3$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_1 = 0.1, \quad \pi_2 = 0.4, \quad \pi_3 = 0.6 \quad$
-   Correlation matrix:

$$\Sigma_D = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0.2} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0.5} \\
\text{0.2} & \text{0.5} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)

n <- 1000
prev <- c(0.1, 0.4, 0.6)

Sigma <- matrix(c(1,   0,   0.2,
                  0,   1,   0.5,
                  0.2, 0.5, 1), 
                3, 3)

dna1 <- simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma)
head(dna1)

# Check prevalences
colMeans(dna1)

# Check dependence (Fisher’s exact tests)
fisher.test(table(dna1$D1, dna1$D2))
fisher.test(table(dna1$D1, dna1$D3))
fisher.test(table(dna1$D2, dna1$D3))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Example 2**

We generate only uncorrelated variables.

-   Sample size: $$n = 1000$$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $$p_D = 3$$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_1 = 0.1, \quad \pi_2 = 0.4, \quad \pi_3 = 0.6 \quad$
-   Correlation matrix:

$$\Sigma_D = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0} \\
\text{0} & \text{0} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
Sigma0 <- diag(3)
dna0 <- simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma0)

# Check prevalences
colMeans(dna0)

# Check dependence (Fisher’s exact tests)
fisher.test(table(dna0$D1, dna0$D2))
fisher.test(table(dna0$D1, dna0$D3))
fisher.test(table(dna0$D2, dna0$D3))
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Example 3**

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

-   Sample size: $n = 1000$
-   Number of DNA pathways: $p_D = 20$
-   Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_{p_D} \sim \mathcal{U}(0.1, 0.6)$
-   Block size: $b_D = 5$
-   Number of blocks: $K_D = 4$
-   Within-block correlations: $\rho_{D;k1} = 0.6, \quad \rho_{D;k2} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{D;k3} = 0.4, \quad \rho_{D;k4} = 0.1$
-   Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B_D} = 0.05$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p <- 20 

# Generate random prevalences 
prev <- runif(20, 0.1, 0.6)
prev
# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.1), 
                             rho_between = 0.05)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("D",1:p)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("D",1:p)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("D",1:p)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("D",1:p)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Simulate correlated data using this matrix 
dna_block <- simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma)

# Check marginal prevalences
data.frame(true_prev = prev, 
           est_prev = colMeans(dna_block))%>%
  rownames_to_column("variable")%>%
  kable(format = "html", caption ="True and empirical prevalences", 
        col.names = c("Variable","True prevalence","Estimated prevalence"))

# 20x20 matrix of raw p-values
pval_block <- pairwise_fisher(dna_block, names(dna_block))

# Heatmap of raw p-values
mat_long <- as.data.frame(as.table(pval_block$p.value.matrix))%>%
  mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq))
ggplot(mat_long, aes(Var1, Var2, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(fill = "Raw p-value", x ="", y = "", 
       title = "Heatmap of raw p-values")
```

# RNA pathways 

**Example 1**

We generate a simple example. 

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways: $p_R = 3$
* Correlation matrix: 

$$\Sigma_R = \left(
\begin{array}{ccc}
\text{1} & \text{0} & \text{0.2} \\
\text{0} & \text{1} & \text{0.5} \\
\text{0.2} & \text{0.5} & \text{1}
\end{array}
\right)$$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p <- 3 
mu <- 0
Sigma <- matrix(c(1,   0,   0.2,
                  0,   1,   -0.5,
                  0.2, -0.5, 1), 
                3, 3)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:p)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:p)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:p)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:p)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

rna1 <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma)

# Check mean 
colMeans(rna1)

# Check variance
apply(rna1, 2, var)

# Correlation matrix
as.data.frame(cor(rna1))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Correlation matrix of simulated variables")
  }
```

---

**Example 2**

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways $p_R = 20$
* Block size: $b_R = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K_R = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{R;k1} = 0.7, \quad \rho_{R;k2} = -0.2, \quad \rho_{R;k3} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{R;k4} = 0.1$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B_R} = 0.05$

```{r}
set.seed(2026)
p = 20 

# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.7, -0.2, 0.5, 0.1), 
                             rho_between = 0.05)
# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:20)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Simulate correlated data using this matrix 
rna_block <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma)

# Check mean 
colMeans(rna_block)

# Check variance 
apply(rna_block, 2, var)

# Correlation matrix
as.data.frame(cor(rna_block))%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Correlation matrix of simulated variables")
  }
```

# Aalen model 

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)

# Sample size 
n <- 1000

# Generate covariates
p <- 3
Sigma <- matrix(c(1, 0, 0,
                  0, 1, 0,
                  0, 0, 1), 
                3, 3)
X <- as.matrix(simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu = 0, Sigma))

# Generate time to event data
beta <- c(0, 0.02, 0.06)  
lambda0 <- 0.07
lambda_i <- lambda0 + drop(X %*% beta)
lambda_i <- pmax(lambda_i, 1e-8)
T <- rexp(n, rate = lambda_i)
cens_rate <- 0.05
C <- rexp(n, rate = cens_rate)
time <- pmin(T, C)
event <- as.integer(T <= C)
data <- data.frame(time, event, X)

# Fit the Aalen model
# All components of the model have nonparametric time-varying effects.
aalen_fit <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~R1+R2+R3, 
                            data = data)
summary(aalen_fit)

# Plot the non-parametric cumulative regression coefficients with 95% CI
par(mfrow=c(1,4))
plot(aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,4))

# Fit the model with the effect of R1 being constant
aalen_fit2 <- timereg::aalen(Surv(time, event)~const(R1)+R2+R3, 
                            data = data)
summary(aalen_fit2)
par(mfrow=c(1,3))
plot(aalen_fit2, ylim = c(-0.5,4))
```

# Cox-Aalen model

```{r}
# Set seed for reproducibility 
set.seed(2026)

# Sample size 
n <- 1000

# Generate covariates
p <- 2
prev <- c(0.2, 0.5)
Sigma <- matrix(c(1, 0,
                  0, 1), 
                p, p)
X_D <- simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma)
X_R <- simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu = 0, Sigma)
X <- cbind(X_D, X_R)

# Generate time to event data
beta_add <- c(0, 0.04)  
beta_mult <- c(0.7, 0)
lambda0 <- 0.05
cens_rate <- 0.05
hazard_i <- lambda0 * exp(drop(as.matrix(X_D) %*% beta_mult))+
  drop(as.matrix(X_R) %*% beta_add)
hazard_i <- pmax(hazard_i, 1e-6)
T <- rexp(n, rate = hazard_i)
C <- rexp(n, rate = cens_rate)
time <- pmin(T, C)
event <- as.integer(T<=C)
data <- data.frame(time, event, X_D, X_R)

# Fit Cox Aalen model
cox_aalen_fit <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~prop(D1)+prop(D2)+R1+R2, 
  data)
summary(cox_aalen_fit)

# Proportional excess terms of model 
cox(cox_aalen_fit)
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(cox_aalen_fit,score= TRUE) 

# Plot additive Aalen terms
par(mfrow = c(1,3))
plot(cox_aalen_fit, ylim = c(-0.5,5))

# Allow some additive terms to be time-constant 
cox_aalen_fit2 <- timereg::cox.aalen(
  Surv(time, event)~prop(D1)+prop(D2)+const(R1)+R2, 
  data, robust = TRUE)
summary(cox_aalen_fit2)

# Proportional excess terms of model 
cox(cox_aalen_fit2)
par(mfrow=c(1,2))
plot(cox_aalen_fit2,score= TRUE) 

# Plot additive Aalen terms
par(mfrow = c(1,3))
plot(cox_aalen_fit2, ylim = c(-0.5, 5)) 
```

# Simulations

All simulations were repeated across $B=1000$ replications. 

## DNA pathways 

For each replicate, pairwise associations between DNA-based pathways were tested using Fisher's exact test, and the empirical type I error was estimated as the proportion of replications with p-values below the nominal significance level ($\alpha=0.05$)

We simulate correlated variables grouped into blocks.

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of DNA pathways: $p_D = 20$
* Prevalence of DNA pathways: $\pi_{p_D} \sim \mathcal{U}(0.1, 0.6)$
* Block size: $b_D = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K_D = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{D;k1} = 0.6, \quad \rho_{D;k2} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{D;k3} = 0.4, \quad \rho_{D;k4} = 0.05$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B_D} = 0.01$

```{r eval=FALSE}
set.seed(2026)
B <- 1000
n <- 1000
p <- 20
prev <- runif(p, 0.1, 0.6) %>%
  set_names(paste0("D",1:p))
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.05),
                             rho_between = 0.01)

# Inspect the matrix 
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("D",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("D",1:20)
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("D",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("D",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Run simulations
dna_sims <- replicate(B,
                      simulate_dna_pathways(n, prev, Sigma),
                      simplify = FALSE) %>%
  imap(~ mutate(.x, B = .y)) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Prevalence bias 
dna_sims %>%
  bind_rows()%>%
  pivot_longer(-B, names_to = "pathway", values_to = "est_prev") %>%
  dplyr::full_join(rownames_to_column(data.frame(true_prev = prev), 
                                      "pathway"), by ="pathway")%>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  summarize(bias = estimate_bias(true = true_prev, est = est_prev),
            pathway = factor(pathway, levels = paste0("D", 1:20)),
            .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = pathway, y = bias, colour = pathway))+
      geom_point(aes(fill = "pathway"))+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed")+
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 0.05))+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Bias", title = "Prevalence bias")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

# Type I error 
dna_sims_pval <- dna_sims %>%
  group_split(B) %>%
  map(~.x %>% 
        dplyr::select(-B)%>%
        pairwise_fisher(vars = names(.)))

# Heatmap of mean raw p-values
dna_sims_pval %>%
  map(~as.data.frame(as.table(.x$p.value.matrix))%>%
        mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq)))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows, .id = "B")%>%
  group_by(Var1, Var2)%>%
  summarise(mean_p.value = mean(Freq), .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(Var1, Var2, fill = mean_p.value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Mean raw p-value", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Heatmap of mean raw p-values")
  }

# Heatmap of the proportion of replications with p-values < 0.05
dna_sims_pval %>%
  map(~as.data.frame(as.table(.x$p.value.matrix))%>%
        mutate(Freq = ifelse(Var1==Var2, NA, Freq)))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows, .id = "B")%>%
  group_by(Var1, Var2)%>%
  summarise(mean_typeI = mean(Freq < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), 
            .groups = "drop")%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(Var1, Var2, fill = mean_typeI)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C", direction = -1) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Proportion of replications \nwith p-values < 0.05", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Heatmap of the proportion of replications with p-values < 0.05")
  }
```

## RNA pathways 

For each pathway, we computed the empirical mean, variance, and pairwise correlation coefficients of the RNA pathways across simulation replicates. 
Mean, variance and correlation coefficients were averaged across replicates. 

* Sample size: $n = 1000$
* Number of RNA pathways $p_R = 20$
* Block size: $b_R = 5$
* Number of blocks: $K_R = 4$
* Within-block correlations: $\rho_{R;k1} = 0.7, \quad \rho_{R;k2} = -0.2, \quad \rho_{R;k3} = 0.5, \quad \rho_{R;k4} = 0.3$
* Between-block correlation: $\rho_{B_R} = 0.1$

```{r eval=FALSE}
set.seed(2026)
p <- 20 
mu <- 0

# Initialize the matrix
Sigma <- generate_block_corr(p, 
                             block_size = 5, 
                             rho_within = c(0.7, -0.2, 0.5, 0.3), 
                             rho_between = 0.1)
colnames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:20)
rownames(Sigma) <- paste0("R",1:20)

# Run simulations 
rna_sims <- replicate(B,
                      simulate_rna_pathways(n, p, mu, Sigma),
                      simplify = FALSE)

# Check mean 
rna_sims %>%
  map(~colMeans(.x))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows)%>%
  summarise_all(mean)%>%
  pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = name, y = value, colour = name))+
      geom_point()+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed")+
      scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.025, 0.025))+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Empirical mean", 
           title = "Empirical mean across replicates")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }

# Check variance
rna_sims %>%
  map(~ apply(.x, 2, var))%>%
  reduce(bind_rows)%>%
  summarise_all(mean)%>%
  pivot_longer(everything())%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(x = name, y = value, colour = name))+
      geom_point()+
      geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed")+
      labs(x = "Pathway", y = "Average variance", 
           title = "Average variance across replicates")+
      theme_minimal()+
      theme(legend.position = "none")
  }
```

```{r eval=FALSE}
#| layout-nrow: 2

# True correlation matrix 
Sigma %>% 
  as.data.frame()%>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
    mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
           var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:20)),
           var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:20)))%>%
    {
      ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
        geom_tile() +
        scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                             limits = c(-1,1)) +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
        labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
             title = "True correlation matrix in the simulation design")
    }

# Average correlation matrix across simulation replicates
cor_sims_rna <- rna_sims %>%
  map(cor) %>%
  reduce(`+`) / length(rna_sims)

as.data.frame(cor_sims_rna)%>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("var1")%>%
  pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2")%>%
  mutate(value = ifelse(var1==var2, NA, value),
         var1 = factor(var1, levels = paste0("R",1:20)),
         var2 = factor(var2, levels = paste0("R",1:20)))%>%
  {
    ggplot(., aes(var1, var2, fill = value)) +
      geom_tile() +
      scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu", direction = -1, 
                           limits = c(-1,1)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1)) +
      labs(fill = "Correlation coefficient", x ="", y = "", 
           title = "Average correlation matrix across simulation replicates")
  }
```
